title: lycopodium friburgense
intro: Lycopodium friburgense is a species of club moss, a type of ancient, non-vascular plant that belongs to the family Lycopodiaceae. It is commonly known for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Lycopodium friburgense is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or powder, which can be used in herbal medicine to promote healing and well-being.
